title: spergula ramosa
intro: Spergula ramosa, also known as corral or wormseed, is a plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea, dysentery, and flatulence. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Spergula ramosa is often used in the form of infusions, teas, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
